body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's User-observable signals are token counts accumulating across turns: `TotalTokens > 0` after turn 1, `TotalTokens` non-decreasing after turn 2, and cache-read tokens reflected for a cached turn. Pi's JSONL transcript carries per-turn `usage.input` / `usage.output` / `usage.cacheRead` / `usage.cacheWrite` plus `cost` on each assistant event, which the pi parser extracts into a per-turn UsageBreakdown and accumulates monotonically into SessionMetrics.TotalTokens. Single-turn token & cost extraction is already exercised by basic-turn; this cell locks down the MULTI-turn accumulator (the non-decreasing claim across requestId boundaries).

The accumulation requires more than one turn on one live session, so the cell is recorded with the INTERACTIVE pi driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh): three back-to-back `Reply with exactly: ok` no-op turns in one tmux REPL via send+wait_turn, then a single teardown. Headless `pi --print` is single-shot (one reply → process_exited) so it cannot accumulate across turn boundaries; the interactive driver keeps the rollout alive for all three turns. The daemon observes a transient `proc-<PID>` pre-session born ready, the UUID-keyed rollout's `transcript_new`, three `working→ready` arcs (one per prompt) on the rollout session, `pid_discovered`, and one `process_exited` at teardown.

The TotalTokens accumulation itself is NOT expressible in the phase DSL today (the invariant DSL has no metric form — a `TotalTokens > 0` assertion is not a phase). It is covered by the pi parser unit tests. The expected.jsonl therefore asserts only the observable lifecycle scaffolding the accounting rides on (the standard three-turn arc, no waiting, a single process_exited).
caveats: Pi cannot accept a pre-assigned UUID — it generates one itself. The interactive driver discovers the UUID from the session header on line 1 of the freshly-created rollout transcript.
The token-accounting claim (TotalTokens > 0 / non-decreasing / cache-read reflected) is not phase-expressible; it is covered by the pi parser unit tests, not by the recording's expected.jsonl phases.

Cache-read counts vary run-to-run (cold-start cache vs warm), and per-turn token counts vary with model-output variance; only the structural three-turn arc, single process_exited, and final ready state are stable across re-records.

sources.note: Identity.Name=pi, Source variant FilesUnderRoot under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/, ExactName{Name: "pi"} process matcher.
Extracts usage.input/output/cacheRead/cacheWrite + cost per assistant event into a per-turn UsageBreakdown; accumulates into SessionMetrics.TotalTokens.
Interactive tmux REPL driver supporting send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep; keeps the rollout session alive across multiple turns so token totals accumulate.
